channel.title: WNwildNews
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from WNwildNews, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: University Lab Worker Admits to Poisoning Colleague in Promotion Dispute
item.description: Makoto Kuroda, 41, a University of Wisconsin lab worker, has admitted to attempting to poison a colleague following a dispute over a workplace promotion.

item.title: France Suspects Weather Sensors Were Tampered With to Influence Polymarket Bets
item.description: French meteorological authorities believe temperature sensors were deliberately manipulated to generate abnormal readings that allowed certain bettors to profit on Polymarket.

item.title: Heavily Filtered Social Media Photos Complicated Search for Missing Mexican Woman
item.description: Police searching for a missing woman in Mexico found that her heavily edited social media images bore little resemblance to her actual appearance, hindering identification efforts.

item.title: Rare Corpse Flower Prepares to Bloom at Kew Gardens Conservatory
item.description: The titan arum, known for its pungent odor, is nearing bloom at the Princess of Wales Conservatory in Kew Gardens, drawing anticipation from botanists and visitors.

item.title: World's Largest Snack Store Stocks Over 35,000 Products from 70 Countries
item.description: Snack Kingdom, spanning more than 12,000 square meters, claims the title of the world's largest snack retail space, offering tens of thousands of products from around the globe.
